title: Rich Slovakian Ground Beef Dumplings
description: A traditional Slovakian dish featuring ground beef and a rich, silky sauce, made without onions or garlic, and finished with a flambe touch.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the ground beef, flour, eggs, milk, salt, black pepper, and paprika, and mix until just combined.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and reduce the heat to a simmer.
Using a spoon, drop the dumpling mixture into the simmering water, making about 12 dumplings.
Cook the dumplings for 10 minutes, or until they float to the surface.
While the dumplings are cooking, melt 2 tablespoons of butter in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the beef broth to the skillet and bring to a simmer.
Using a slotted spoon, remove the dumplings from the water and add them to the skillet with the sauce.
Reduce the heat to low and let the dumplings simmer in the sauce for 5 minutes.
Add the cognac to the skillet and ignite the sauce, being careful not to burn yourself.
Let the sauce flambe for 30 seconds, then extinguish the flames.
Serve the dumplings hot, topped with the rich and silky sauce.
